overview.title: GraphQL API Overview
overview.description: The GraphQL API provides a flexible, typed interface for interacting with the orders platform.

sections.title: HTTP Method
sections.content: All GraphQL operations use **POST** with `Content-Type: application/json`. The request body must contain `query` (string), `variables` (object, optional) and `operationName` (string, optional).

items.title: No Over-fetching
items.text: Request exactly the fields you need, nothing more.

items.title: Typed Schema
items.text: SDL schema with well-defined and documented types.

items.title: Subscriptions
items.text: Real-time updates via WebSocket for order events.

items.title: Introspection
items.text: Explore the full schema via introspection in the Playground.

sections.title: Request Example
sections.code:
curl -X POST https://api.example.com/graphql \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer {access_token}" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"query": "{ orders(status: PENDING) { id status customer { name } } }"}'

authentication.title: Authentication
authentication.description: The GraphQL API shares the same OAuth 2.0 authentication system as the REST API.

sections.title: Same Credentials
sections.content: Use the same `client_id` and `client_secret` as the REST API. See the **Authentication** section in the REST docs for the complete token flow.

query-orders.title: Query: orders
query-orders.description: Returns a list of orders with support for filters and pagination.

sections.title: GraphQL Query

query-order.title: Query: order
query-order.description: Returns the complete details of a specific order by ID.

sections.title: GraphQL Query

confirm-order.title: Mutation: confirmOrder
confirm-order.description: Confirms receipt of an order. Must be called within 5 minutes.

sections.title: GraphQL Mutation

update-order-status.title: Mutation: updateOrderStatus
update-order-status.description: Updates the status of a confirmed order.
